completion_promises.evidence: Created src/forge/pipeline/per_feature_sequencer.py defining PerFeatureLoopSequencer. Verified by TestPerFeatureLoopSequencerExists::test_class_importable_from_per_feature_sequencer_module and ::test_class_is_instantiable_with_no_arguments — both pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Method may_start_autobuild(build_id, feature_id, stage_log_reader, async_task_reader) -> bool returns False if any prior feature's autobuild is in a non-terminal lifecycle (starting, planning_waves, running_wave, awaiting_approval, pushing_pr)
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented may_start_autobuild with the exact signature. The NON_TERMINAL_AUTOBUILD_LIFECYCLES frozenset constant lists the five literals verbatim from the criterion. Parametrized test TestNonTerminalLifecyclesBlockDispatch::test_prior_feature_in_non_terminal_lifecycle_blocks_second_feature exercises all five lifecycle values and asserts the method returns False — all five parametrizations pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Method consults both stage_log (terminal completion) and the async_tasks state channel via async_task_reader (live lifecycle, DDR-006 AutobuildState)
completion_promises.evidence: may_start_autobuild walks async_task_reader.list_autobuild_states(build_id) for live lifecycle and calls stage_log_reader.is_autobuild_approved(build_id, feature_id) when the async_tasks lifecycle is non-terminal — implementing DDR-006's 'SQLite is authoritative, async_tasks is advisory' rule. Test TestStageLogAndAsyncTaskConsultation::test_stale_async_task_overridden_by_stage_log_approval verifies the cross-consultation: a non-terminal async_tasks entry whose feature is also marked approved in stage_log does not block dispatch.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Returns True when no autobuild for any earlier feature is non-terminal
completion_promises.evidence: may_start_autobuild falls through the for-loop and returns True when no sibling autobuild matches the block conditions. Tests TestStageLogAndAsyncTaskConsultation::test_no_in_flight_autobuilds_permits_dispatch (empty channel) and TestMultiFeatureCatalogue::test_all_prior_features_completed_permits_third_feature (three-feature catalogue with two prior completions) both verify the True path.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit test: two-feature catalogue, second autobuild dispatch blocked while first autobuild lifecycle is running_wave
completion_promises.evidence: TestNonTerminalLifecyclesBlockDispatch::test_running_wave_lifecycle_blocks_second_feature_two_feature_catalogue sets up a two-feature catalogue with FEAT-1 in running_wave and asserts may_start_autobuild for FEAT-2 returns False.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit test: second autobuild dispatch permitted once first reaches completed
completion_promises.evidence: TestTerminalLifecyclesPermitDispatch::test_completed_lifecycle_permits_second_feature_two_feature_catalogue sets FEAT-1's lifecycle to completed and stage_log to approved; asserts may_start_autobuild for FEAT-2 returns True. The parametrized test in the same class also covers cancelled and failed terminal states.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Pure function — no I/O except via injected reader Protocols
completion_promises.evidence: The module imports only `typing.Iterable`, `typing.Protocol`, and `typing.runtime_checkable` from the stdlib — no sqlite3, requests, aiohttp, or forge.adapters. TestPureFunctionContract::test_no_module_level_io_imports parses the module's AST and asserts no Import / ImportFrom node references any forbidden I/O prefix; ::test_protocols_are_runtime_checkable asserts both StageLogReader and AsyncTaskReader are runtime_checkable Protocols satisfied by the in-memory test doubles.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: No lint or format tooling (ruff, black, flake8, mypy) is configured in pyproject.toml — the project ships only pytest configuration. Both new files are written to PEP 8 conventions: 88-char line cap (the .claude/rules/code-style.md target), snake_case functions, PascalCase classes, UPPER_CASE constants, full type hints, f-strings, dataclasses for state containers, no print statements. There is therefore no configured lint check to fail; the AC is vacuously satisfied for the project's current toolchain.
